title: [arcilator] Failed to legalize 'sim.clocked_terminate' and dangling macro references
body:
### Describe the bug

When using arcilator to compile MLIR generated by recent versions of firtool (which now emits sim.clocked_terminate for stop() and assert() statements), the compilation fails with two consecutive issues:

Undefined Symbol Error: The StripSV pass removes sv.macro.decl but leaves sv.macro.ref.expr (e.g., @STOP_COND_) untouched if they are used as operands for sim.clocked_terminate outside of an sv.always block.

Legalization Failure: After manually resolving the symbol issue, the lower-arc-to-llvm pass fails to legalize sim.clocked_terminate, as there are no lowering patterns for the sim dialect in arcilator.

### Steps to Reproduce

Create a file `reproduce.mlir`:

```
module {
  sv.macro.decl @STOP_COND_
  
  hw.module @SimTop(in %clock: !seq.clock, in %cond: i1) {
    %STOP_COND_ = sv.macro.ref.expr @STOP_COND_() : () -> i1
    %1 = comb.and bin %STOP_COND_, %cond : i1
    
    sim.clocked_terminate %clock, %1, success, verbose
  }
}
```
Run arcilator:

```bash
arcilator reproduce.mlir
```

### Expected Behavior
arcilator should be able to handle sim.clocked_terminate by lowering it to a C-style exit() or a similar simulation-ending runtime call, and it should correctly handle macro references used in these contexts.

### Actual Behavior
The compiler crashes with:

error: references an undefined symbol: @STOP_COND_ (in StripSV)

If symbols are fixed: error: failed to legalize operation 'sim.clocked_terminate' (in lower-arc-to-llvm)

### Additional Context

I have a pending PR #10086 to fix the first issue (replacing i1 condition macros with constants in StripSV). However, the second issue (missing lowering for sim.clocked_terminate) requires a proper lowering pattern in LowerArcToLLVM.cpp.

This issue is critical for users moving to newer firtool versions, as it breaks all designs containing stop() or assert() when using arcilator.
